heading: Reporting of child abuse
label: 1169 Reporting of child abuse

L. 103–322, title XXXIII, § 330011(d)108 Stat. 2144Pub. L. 104–294, title VI, § 604(b)(25)110 Stat. 3508section 404(a) of Pub. L. 101–630Sept. 13, 1994Oct. 11, 1996

children.label: A
children.text: physician, surgeon, dentist, podiatrist, chiropractor, nurse, dental hygienist, optometrist, medical examiner, emergency medical technician, paramedic, or health care provider,

children.label: B
children.text: teacher, school counselor, instructional aide, teacher’s aide, teacher’s assistant, or bus driver employed by any tribal, Federal, public or private school,

children.label: C
children.text: administrative officer, supervisor of child welfare and attendance, or truancy officer of any tribal, Federal, public or private school,

children.label: D
children.text: child day care worker, headstart teacher, public assistance worker, worker in a group home or residential or day care facility, or social worker,

children.label: E
children.text: psychiatrist, psychologist, or psychological assistant,

children.label: F
children.text: licensed or unlicensed marriage, family, or child counselor,

children.label: G
children.text: person employed in the mental health profession, or

children.label: H
children.text: law enforcement officer, probation officer, worker in a juvenile rehabilitation or detention facility, or person employed in a public agency who is responsible for enforcing statutes and judicial orders;

children.label: A
children.text: a child was abused in Indian country, or

children.label: B
children.text: actions are being taken, or are going to be taken, that would reasonably be expected to result in abuse of a child in Indian country; and
fails to immediately report such abuse or actions described in paragraph (2) to the local child protective services agency or local law enforcement agency,
supervises, or has authority over, a person described in subsection (a)(1), and
inhibits or prevents that person from making the report described in subsection (a),

children.label: i
children.text: a child is dead or exhibits evidence of skin bruising, bleeding, malnutrition, failure to thrive, burns, fracture of any bone, subdural hematoma, soft tissue swelling, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such condition is not justifiably explained or may not be the product of an accidental occurrence; and

children.label: B
children.text: any case in which a child is subjected to sexual assault, sexual molestation, sexual exploitation, sexual contact, or prostitution;

children.label: A
children.text: is not married, and

children.label: B
children.text: has not attained 18 years of age;

“local child protective services agency” means that agency of the Federal Government, of a State, or of an Indian tribe that has the primary responsibility for child protection on any Indian reservation or within any community in Indian country; and

“local law enforcement agency” means that Federal, tribal, or State law enforcement agency that has the primary responsibility for the investigation of an instance of alleged child abuse within the portion of Indian country involved.

children.label: d
children.text: Any person making a report described in subsection (a) which is based upon their reasonable belief and which is made in good faith shall be immune from civil or criminal liability for making that report.
